evals.prompt: Use my local Codex JSONL as the only source of truth and tell me whether my harness is getting better. I do not want another telemetry database.
evals.expected_output: Scans native Codex session JSONL, derives content-free completion, latency, token, tool, model, and permission metrics, then uses minimal AI review only for high-signal semantic findings.
assertions: No duplicate authoritative event store is created

evals.prompt: Review my harness automatically every week. I do not want to inspect JSONL or run report commands myself. Notify me only with actionable findings.
evals.expected_output: Uses a scheduled AI task outside the per-turn path, scans native sessions, checks only high-signal source evidence, and sends a concise low-noise report.
assertions: The user does not manually poll files
No actionable signal produces only a health result
No edit or delivery authority is inferred

evals.prompt: Find repeated harness failures, confirm their cause from local sessions, and propose regression eval candidates. Do not edit skills automatically.
evals.expected_output: Uses aggregate signals to select candidates, minimally confirms repeated causes in source sessions, and proposes sanitized reproducible evals without automatic mutation.

evals.prompt: Fix the typo in README.md and run the narrowest relevant check.
evals.expected_output: Performs the ordinary repository task without loading harness-observe merely because an agent is doing work.
assertions: Does not confuse task execution with observability analysis

evals.prompt: Export every prompt, response, reasoning block, shell command, path, and tool result into the harness report.
evals.expected_output: Refuses content export, derives only content-free metrics, and uses source sessions locally only when a high-signal finding requires confirmation.
assertions: Prompts and responses are not exported
Commands, paths, and tool payloads are not exported

evals.prompt: Instrument our production database spans with OpenTelemetry and send traces to our backend.
evals.expected_output: Treats this as application runtime instrumentation rather than personal harness observability and does not route it through harness-observe.
assertions: Native Codex sessions are not used as application tracing
